reasoning: The most recent known value of the US Treasury General Account (D2WLTGAL) was highly elevated at over $905 billion. Historically, the Treasury targets a level around $750-$850 billion, suggesting that over an extended timeframe, mean reversion will pull the balance downward as funds are disbursed. Thus, the probability of an increase from the November 9, 2025 baseline is below 50%. As per the instructions and the nature of random-walk-like volatility over longer horizons, uncertainty increases over time, pulling forecasts for more distant resolution dates closer to 0.5 (42% for 1 week out, 46% for 1 month out, and 49% for 3 months out).
